prp.objective: Create internal/generate/generate.go — the ★ two-nested-loop ★ snapshot-based atomic-commit orchestrator CommitStaged(ctx, deps Deps) (Result, error) — and the two supporting seams it needs: an exported (*provider.Executor).Parse (one-line wrapper over the package-private provider.parseOutput) and a new git.DiffTreeNameStatus(sha) method (the FR42 success print). CommitStaged integrates M2 (provider Run/parse), M3 (git plumbing/diff/log), M4 (prompt builders), M5 (config), and this milestone's S2 (firstLine/isDuplicate), S3 (Rescue), and T2.S1 (installSignalHandler/restoreSignalHandler). It drives diff→snapshot→prompt→generate(outer dup-rejection loop wrapping inner parse-correction loop)→dedupe→commit-tree→restore-signal→update-ref CAS, returns sentinel errors (ErrNothingToCommit/ErrRescue/ErrHeadMoved) WITHOUT os.Exit, NEVER stages (v2 seam), and prints the FR42 success block. This is the most important subtask in the plan (decisions.md §3, reference_impl.md §2). All collaborators arrive via the single injectable Deps value so everything is stubbable for hermetic unit tests.
prp.context:
## Goal

**Feature Goal**: A tested, self-contained `internal/generate/generate.go` exposing `CommitStaged(ctx, deps) (Result, error)` — the product's defensibility core: the two-nested-loop (outer duplicate-rejection wrapping inner parse-correction) snapshot-based atomic-commit orchestrator that integrates M2+M3+M4+M5+M6.S2/S3/T2.S1, returns typed sentinel errors (no os.Exit), and NEVER stages (v2 seam).

**Deliverable**: (a) `internal/generate/generate.go` — owns `// Package generate`, defines consumer-side `gitClient`+`runner` interfaces (so `*git.Git`/`*provider.Executor` satisfy them structurally with ZERO edits to existing types except the two seams below), the `Deps` value, `Result`, three sentinel errors, and `CommitStaged`; (b) an ADDITIVE exported `func (e *Executor) Parse(raw string, m Manifest) (string, bool)` in `internal/provider/executor.go` (one line: `return parseOutput(raw, m)`); (c) a NEW `func (g *Git) DiffTreeNameStatus(sha string) (string, error)` in `internal/git/plumbing.go`; (d) `internal/generate/generate_test.go` — white-box `package generate` unit tests with a stub `gitClient`+`runner`.

**Success Definition**: `go build ./internal/...`, `go vet ./internal/...`, gofmt clean, and `go test ./internal/...` all pass; the two-nested-loop counting (1 initial + N dup-retries, each with a FRESH 2-try inner budget) is exact; every failure path renders Rescue with the correct candidate ("" except dup-exhaustion) and returns the right sentinel; CAS failure prints the §13.5 head-moved message and returns ErrHeadMoved (NEVER forces); signal-handler install/restore are wired at the exact documented call-sites.

## Why

- **Defensibility / core IP**: this is PRD §13 — the snapshot-based atomic-commit core. The two-nested-loop (FR29 inner + FR30–FR33 outer) and the dedupe guarantee are the product's differentiator.
- **Safety invariants (PRD §18.1)**: the repo's refs change ONLY at the final UpdateRefCAS; the index is snapshotted by write-tree BEFORE generation so it can be re-staged concurrently; on any post-snapshot failure the dangling tree is recoverable via Rescue's printed manual command.
- **Integration point**: CommitStaged is consumed by `pkg/stagehand.GenerateCommit` (P1.M7.T1.S1) and the CLI default action (P1.M7.T2.S1); it is asserted end-to-end by the integration + invariant suites (P1.M6.T3.S2/S3) which drive it with the real temp repo + stub agent binary.

## What

### User-visible behavior
- `CommitStaged` captures the staged diff; if empty → `ErrNothingToCommit` (CLI maps to exit 2). It snapshots the index via write-tree (FR8: abort on conflict BEFORE generation), installs the signal handler (post-snapshot), builds the system prompt, fetches recent subjects, runs the two-nested-loop generation, and on a unique subject commits via commit-tree + update-ref CAS.
- On success it prints the FR42 block to stdout (`[<short-sha>] <subject>` then the `git diff-tree --no-commit-id --name-status -r <sha>` lines) and returns `Result{CommitSHA, Subject, Message}`.
- On timeout/agent-error/parse-fail-after-inner/dup-exhaustion it renders Rescue (candidate=msg ONLY on dup-exhaustion) and returns `ErrRescue` (exit 3). On CAS conflict it prints the §13.5 head-moved message + manual recovery and returns `ErrHeadMoved` (exit 1, NEVER force).
- It NEVER calls `git add` (staging policy is CLI-only — v2 seam).

### Success Criteria
- [ ] CommitStaged is `func CommitStaged(ctx context.Context, deps Deps) (Result, error)` taking a single `Deps` value (all collaborators injectable).
- [ ] Two-nested-loop counting is EXACT: outer `for dupAttempt := 0; dupAttempt <= cfg.MaxDuplicateRetries; dupAttempt++` (1 initial + N retries, default 4 total); inner `for parseAttempt := 1; parseAttempt <= 2; parseAttempt++` with a FRESH 2-try budget every outer iteration.
- [ ] Corrective-retry REBUILDS the payload via `prompt.AssemblePayload(diff, instr, rejected)` — the diff (and growing rejected list) STAYS; only the instruction slot swaps to `manifest.RetryInstruction` (non-empty) else the original instruction. The instruction is RESET to the original at the top of each outer iteration.
- [ ] CommitStaged RETURNS sentinel errors (ErrNothingToCommit/ErrRescue/ErrHeadMoved) — it NEVER os.Exit (the CLI maps them via errors.Is to exit codes).
- [ ] Rescue candidate is `msg` ONLY on dup-exhaustion; `""` on timeout/agent-error/parse-fail/post-snapshot git errors. Signal-cancel path returns ErrRescue WITHOUT rendering Rescue (the signal handler already rendered it + exited).
- [ ] WriteTree error is returned directly (NOT ErrRescue — no tree to rescue). UpdateRefCAS failure → §13.5 message + ErrHeadMoved; NEVER `--force`, never 1-arg form except root.
- [ ] restoreSignalHandler() is called immediately BEFORE UpdateRefCAS (Gotcha #1); installSignalHandler immediately AFTER WriteTree.
- [ ] Root commit (RevParseHEAD hasParent=false ⇒ parentSHA="") → CommitTree/UpdateRefCAS called with "" (omit -p, no expected-old).
- [ ] `(*provider.Executor).Parse` added additively to executor.go; `git.DiffTreeNameStatus` added to plumbing.go; generate.go owns `// Package generate`.

## All Needed Context

### Context Completeness Check
Passes the "No Prior Knowledge" test: every dependency signature is quoted verbatim from the shipped code, the five ambiguities are resolved with citations, and the ordered control flow is specified line-by-line. The interfaces make `*git.Git`/`*provider.Executor` satisfy the contracts structurally — NO existing type is modified except the two named additive seams.

## Implementation Blueprint

### Data models and structure

```go
package generate
import (
    "context"
    "errors"
    "github.com/dustin/stagehand/internal/config"
    "github.com/dustin/stagehand/internal/git"
    "github.com/dustin/stagehand/internal/provider"
    "github.com/dustin/stagehand/internal/prompt"
    "github.com/dustin/stagehand/internal/ui"
)
type gitClient interface {
    StagedDiff(git.DiffSettings) (string, error)
    RevParseHEAD() (string, bool, error)
    WriteTree() (string, error)
    CommitTree(parent, msg, tree string) (string, error)
    UpdateRefCAS(ref, newSHA, expected string) error
    CommitCount() (int, error)
    RecentMessages(n int) (string, error)
    RecentSubjects(n int) ([]string, error)
    DiffTreeNameStatus(sha string) (string, error)
}
type runner interface {
    Run(ctx context.Context, m provider.Manifest, model, providerName, sys, payload string) (string, error)
    Parse(raw string, m provider.Manifest) (string, bool)
}
type Deps struct {
    Git      gitClient
    Runner   runner
    Manifest provider.Manifest
    Config   config.Config
    Output   *ui.Output
}
type Result struct {
    CommitSHA string
    Subject   string
    Message   string
}
var (
    ErrNothingToCommit = errors.New("nothing staged to commit")
    ErrRescue          = errors.New("commit generation failed; rescue instructions printed")
    ErrHeadMoved       = errors.New("HEAD moved while generating; aborted to avoid non-fast-forward")
)
func CommitStaged(ctx context.Context, deps Deps) (Result, error) {  }

```

### The ordered control flow (the binding spec — port verbatim)
```go
func CommitStaged(ctx context.Context, deps Deps) (Result, error) {
    cfg, out := deps.Config, deps.Output
    diff, err := deps.Git.StagedDiff(git.DiffSettings{
        MaxMdLines:   cfg.MaxMdLines,
        MaxDiffBytes: cfg.MaxDiffBytes,
    })
    if err != nil { return Result{}, err }                 
    if diff == "" { return Result{}, ErrNothingToCommit }  
    parentSHA, hasParent, err := deps.Git.RevParseHEAD()
    if err != nil { return Result{}, err }
    _ = hasParent 
    treeSHA, err := deps.Git.WriteTree()
    if err != nil { return Result{}, err }                 
    runCtx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
    if cfg.Timeout > 0 {
        runCtx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(runCtx, cfg.Timeout)  
    }
    defer cancel()
    installSignalHandler(cancel, func() {
        if treeSHA != "" { Rescue(out, treeSHA, parentSHA, "") } 
    })
    count, err := deps.Git.CommitCount()
    if err != nil { Rescue(out, treeSHA, parentSHA, ""); return Result{}, ErrRescue }
    examples, hasMultiline, err := prompt.FetchExamples(deps.Git, prompt.DefaultExampleCount)
    if err != nil { Rescue(out, treeSHA, parentSHA, ""); return Result{}, ErrRescue }
    sys := prompt.BuildSystemPrompt(examples, hasMultiline, count <= 1, cfg.SubjectTargetChars)
    subjects, err := deps.Git.RecentSubjects(50)
    if err != nil { Rescue(out, treeSHA, parentSHA, ""); return Result{}, ErrRescue }
    const instruction = "Generate a commit message for these changes:"
    rejected := []string{}
    var msg string
    var ok, committed bool
outer:
    for dupAttempt := 0; dupAttempt <= cfg.MaxDuplicateRetries; dupAttempt++ { 
        instr := instruction                                              
        ok = false
        for parseAttempt := 1; parseAttempt <= 2; parseAttempt++ {        
            payload := prompt.AssemblePayload(diff, instr, rejected)      
            stdout, runErr := deps.Runner.Run(runCtx, deps.Manifest, cfg.Model, cfg.Provider, sys, payload)
            if runErr != nil {
                if errors.Is(runErr, context.Canceled) {
                    return Result{}, ErrRescue                            
                }
                Rescue(out, treeSHA, parentSHA, "")                       
                return Result{}, ErrRescue
            }
            msg, ok = deps.Runner.Parse(stdout, deps.Manifest)
            if ok { break }
            if deps.Manifest.RetryInstruction != "" { instr = deps.Manifest.RetryInstruction } 
        }
        if !ok { Rescue(out, treeSHA, parentSHA, ""); return Result{}, ErrRescue } 
        subject := firstLine(msg)
        if !isDuplicate(subject, subjects) { committed = true; break outer }       
        rejected = append(rejected, subject)                                       
    }
    if !committed { Rescue(out, treeSHA, parentSHA, msg); return Result{}, ErrRescue } 
    newSHA, err := deps.Git.CommitTree(parentSHA, msg, treeSHA)          
    if err != nil { Rescue(out, treeSHA, parentSHA, ""); return Result{}, ErrRescue }
    restoreSignalHandler()                                               
    if err := deps.Git.UpdateRefCAS("HEAD", newSHA, parentSHA); err != nil { 
        out.Progressf("HEAD moved while generating; aborting to avoid a non-fast-forward.\n")
        out.Progressf("Your generated message was: %s\n", msg)
        parentFlag := ""
        if parentSHA != "" { parentFlag = "-p " + parentSHA + " " }       
        out.Progressf("To commit the snapshot manually:\n  git commit-tree %s-m %q %s | xargs git update-ref HEAD\n",
            parentFlag, msg, treeSHA)
        return Result{}, ErrHeadMoved                                    
    }
    short := newSHA
    if len(short) > 7 { short = short[:7] }
    subj := firstLine(msg)
    out.Resultf("[%s] %s\n", short, subj)                                
    if dt, derr := deps.Git.DiffTreeNameStatus(newSHA); derr == nil {
        out.Resultf("%s", dt)                                            
    }
    return Result{CommitSHA: newSHA, Subject: subj, Message: msg}, nil
}

```

### Supporting seam #1 — (*provider.Executor).Parse (ADDITIVE in executor.go)
```go
func (e *Executor) Parse(raw string, m Manifest) (string, bool) { return parseOutput(raw, m) }

```

### Supporting seam #2 — git.DiffTreeNameStatus (NEW in plumbing.go)
```go
func (g *Git) DiffTreeNameStatus(sha string) (string, error) {
    out, err := g.run("diff-tree", "--no-commit-id", "--name-status", "-r", sha)
    if err != nil { return "", err }
    return out, nil 
}

```

  - FOLLOW pattern: the existing Run method's doc-comment density; additive only.

Task 2: ADD internal/git/plumbing.go — DiffTreeNameStatus
  - IMPLEMENT: func (g *Git) DiffTreeNameStatus(sha string) (string, error) running
    `git diff-tree --no-commit-id --name-status -r <sha>` via g.run; return raw stdout.
  - FOLLOW pattern: CommitTree/UpdateRefCAS (thin g.run + errors.As into *ExitError on non-zero).
  - CONSTRAINT: read-only; touches no ref/index. Args are literal (no sh -c, §19).
  - TEST: add a plumbing_test.go case (temp repo + real git, commit a file, assert the name-status
    lines for the resulting SHA). Reuse internal/git/gittestutil_test.go temp-repo helpers.
Task 3: CREATE internal/generate/generate.go
  - OWNS `// Package generate` doc comment (dedupe.go/rescue.go/signal.go defer to it).
  - IMPLEMENT: the gitClient + runner interfaces, Deps, Result, the three sentinel errors
    (ErrNothingToCommit/ErrRescue/ErrHeadMoved), and CommitStaged EXACTLY per the ordered
    control flow above.
  - CONSTRAINTS: NEVER os.Exit; NEVER call git.AddAll/HasStagedChanges (v2 seam); restore signal
    handler BEFORE UpdateRefCAS; install AFTER WriteTree.
  - DEPENDENCIES: Tasks 1+2 (Parse + DiffTreeNameStatus) + shipped S2/S3/T2.S1.
Task 4: CREATE internal/generate/generate_test.go
  - IMPLEMENT: white-box `package generate`; a stubGit (implements gitClient with canned returns +
    a call log) and a stubRunner (Run returns canned stdout/error; Parse returns canned (msg,ok)).
  - FOLLOW pattern: stubprovider_test.go house conventions (stdlib + internal/* only; NO testify;
    t.Run table-driven; ui.NewOutput(&stdout,&stderr,false,true) so Red is a no-op).
  - CASES: happy-path (unique first try); dup-retry-then-success; parse-retry-then-success;
    parse-fail-after-inner (both Parse ok=false) → Rescue(candidate="")+ErrRescue;
    dup-exhaustion (always dup, exactly MaxDuplicateRetries+1 Run calls) → Rescue(candidate=last msg)+ErrRescue;
    nothing-to-commit (diff=="") → ErrNothingToCommit, NO Rescue, NO WriteTree;
    WriteTree error → returned directly (no rescue);
    timeout (Run returns *provider.TimeoutError) → Rescue+ErrRescue;
    signal-cancel (Run returns context.Canceled) → ErrRescue, Rescue NOT called by CommitStaged;
    head-moved (UpdateRefCAS errors) → §13.5 message+ErrHeadMoved; CommitTree WAS called; never force;
    root commit (RevParseHEAD hasParent=false) → CommitTree/UpdateRefCAS called with "".
  - ASSERTIONS: call counts (Run invoked MaxDuplicateRetries+1 times on dup-exhaustion; CommitTree+
    UpdateRefCAS exactly once on success); Rescue stderr presence/absence; candidate substring;
    Result fields; stdout contains `[<short>] <subject>`.
Task 5: RUN validation gates in order and fix every failure before declaring done (see gates).

## Final Validation Checklist
### Technical
- [ ] All 4 validation levels pass (`go build ./internal/...`, `go vet ./internal/...`, gofmt clean, `go test ./...`).
- [ ] `(*provider.Executor).Parse` added additively; parse.go/parse_test.go untouched.
- [ ] `git.DiffTreeNameStatus` added to plumbing.go; read-only; args literal (no sh -c).
- [ ] generate.go owns `// Package generate`; dedupe/rescue/signal untouched (plain package lines).

### Feature
- [ ] Two-nested-loop counting EXACT: outer `dupAttempt<=MaxDuplicateRetries` (inclusive); inner fresh 2-try budget each outer iter.
- [ ] Corrective retry rebuilds payload keeping the diff; instruction slot swaps to RetryInstruction (else original); reset each outer iter.
- [ ] CommitStaged returns sentinels (ErrNothingToCommit/ErrRescue/ErrHeadMoved); NEVER os.Exit.
- [ ] Candidate=msg ONLY on dup-exhaustion; "" otherwise; signal-cancel returns ErrRescue WITHOUT rendering Rescue.
- [ ] restoreSignalHandler BEFORE UpdateRefCAS; install AFTER WriteTree.
- [ ] Root commit: CommitTree/UpdateRefCAS called with ""; never --force; never 1-arg except root.
- [ ] FR42 success block printed to stdout; Result{CommitSHA,Subject,Message} returned.

### Code Quality
- [ ] Follows existing patterns (thin g.run + errors.As; Rescue pure-render; house test conventions).
- [ ] No testify / external deps; stdlib + internal/* only.
- [ ] Scope respected: NEVER git add; never os.Exit; never modify parse.go/manifest.go.

### Documentation & Deployment
- [ ] Code is self-documenting (dense doc comments matching sibling files).
- [ ] Docs: this task has no Mode-A per-item DOCS line; defers to Mode-B changeset-level doc sync in M8. No doc file edit required in this task.

## Anti-Patterns to Avoid
- ❌ Don't flatten the two loops into one retry counter — the nesting (fresh inner budget per outer iter) is the contract.
- ❌ Don't drop the diff on corrective retry (Ambiguity #2) — rebuild via AssemblePayload(diff,instr,rejected).
- ❌ Don't call provider.parseOutput directly (package-private) — go through the (*Executor).Parse seam.
- ❌ Don't os.Exit from CommitStaged — return sentinels; the CLI maps exit codes.
- ❌ Don't restore the signal handler AFTER UpdateRefCAS (Gotcha #1) — BEFORE.
- ❌ Don't render Rescue twice on the signal path — errors.Is(err,context.Canceled) ⇒ return ErrRescue without Rescue.
- ❌ Don't call git add/AddAll/HasStagedChanges (v2 seam — staging is CLI-only).
- ❌ Don't force update-ref or use the 1-arg form except the root commit.
- ❌ Don't add testify or external deps.
- ❌ Don't create the package doc comment on a sibling — generate.go OWNS it.
implementationSteps: ADD to internal/provider/executor.go an exported one-line method `func (e *Executor) Parse(raw string, m Manifest) (string, bool) { return parseOutput(raw, m) }`. This exposes the package-private parseOutput (PRD §12.9) to the cross-package generate orchestrator. Do NOT modify parse.go or parse_test.go. Add a doc comment matching Run's density explaining why the seam exists (parseOutput is package-private; generate is a different package) and that it stays a thin pass-through so parse_test.go remains white-box.

ADD to internal/git/plumbing.go a new method `func (g *Git) DiffTreeNameStatus(sha string) (string, error)` that runs `git diff-tree --no-commit-id --name-status -r <sha>` via the unexported g.run seam, surfaces a non-zero exit as the typed *git.ExitError (errors.As, like CommitTree/UpdateRefCAS), and returns the RAW stdout (trailing newline intact; caller prints verbatim). Document it as read-only (touches no ref/index) and as the FR42 success-print query. Add a plumbing_test.go case using the gittestutil_test.go temp-repo helpers: commit a file, assert the name-status lines for the resulting SHA.

CREATE internal/generate/generate.go as a plain `package generate` file that OWNS the `// Package generate` doc comment (dedupe.go/rescue.go/signal.go already defer to it via plain package lines — do NOT change them). Imports: context, errors; internal/config, internal/git, internal/provider, internal/prompt, internal/ui.

In generate.go DEFINE the consumer-side interfaces `gitClient` (StagedDiff(git.DiffSettings); RevParseHEAD(); WriteTree(); CommitTree(parent,msg,tree); UpdateRefCAS(ref,new,expected); CommitCount(); RecentMessages(n); RecentSubjects(n); DiffTreeNameStatus(sha)) and `runner` (Run(ctx,provider.Manifest,model,providerName,sys,payload); Parse(string,provider.Manifest)) — so *git.Git and *provider.Executor satisfy them STRUCTURALLY with no edits beyond Tasks 1+2. Because gitClient embeds CommitCount+RecentMessages it also satisfies prompt.HistoryReader.

In generate.go DEFINE `type Deps struct { Git gitClient; Runner runner; Manifest provider.Manifest; Config config.Config; Output *ui.Output }`, `type Result struct { CommitSHA, Subject, Message string }`, and the three package-level sentinel errors via errors.New: ErrNothingToCommit, ErrRescue, ErrHeadMoved. Document that CommitStaged RETURNS these (never os.Exit) and the CLI (P1.M7.T2) maps them to exit codes.

In generate.go IMPLEMENT `func CommitStaged(ctx context.Context, deps Deps) (Result, error)` EXACTLY per the ordered control flow: (1) diff via deps.Git.StagedDiff(git.DiffSettings{MaxMdLines:cfg.MaxMdLines, MaxDiffBytes:cfg.MaxDiffBytes}); empty→ErrNothingToCommit (no rescue). (2) parentSHA from RevParseHEAD; treeSHA from WriteTree (error returned directly — no rescue). (3) runCtx=WithTimeout(ctx,cfg.Timeout) guarded on cfg.Timeout>0; installSignalHandler(cancel, func(){ if treeSHA!="" { Rescue(out,treeSHA,parentSHA,"") } }). (4) count→FetchExamples(deps.Git, prompt.DefaultExampleCount)→BuildSystemPrompt(examples,hasMultiline,count<=1,cfg.SubjectTargetChars)→RecentSubjects(50); any error→Rescue("",)+ErrRescue.

In CommitStaged IMPLEMENT the two nested loops verbatim: outer `for dupAttempt:=0; dupAttempt<=cfg.MaxDuplicateRetries; dupAttempt++` (labeled `outer:`) with `instr:=instruction` reset each iteration and `ok=false`; inner `for parseAttempt:=1; parseAttempt<=2; parseAttempt++` doing payload=prompt.AssemblePayload(diff,instr,rejected); stdout,runErr:=deps.Runner.Run(runCtx,deps.Manifest,cfg.Model,cfg.Provider,sys,payload). On runErr: errors.Is(runErr,context.Canceled)→return Result{},ErrRescue (NO Rescue — handler did it); else Rescue(out,treeSHA,parentSHA,"")+ErrRescue. Then msg,ok=deps.Runner.Parse(stdout,deps.Manifest); if ok break; if deps.Manifest.RetryInstruction!="" { instr=deps.Manifest.RetryInstruction }.

Finish the loops: after the inner loop, if !ok → Rescue(out,treeSHA,parentSHA,"")+ErrRescue. subject=firstLine(msg); if !isDuplicate(subject,subjects) { committed=true; break outer }; else rejected=append(rejected,subject). After the outer loop, if !committed → Rescue(out,treeSHA,parentSHA,msg)+ErrRescue (dup-exhaustion, candidate=msg).

In CommitStaged IMPLEMENT the COMMIT block: newSHA,err:=deps.Git.CommitTree(parentSHA,msg,treeSHA) (omits -p when parentSHA==""); on err → Rescue(out,treeSHA,parentSHA,"")+ErrRescue. restoreSignalHandler() IMMEDIATELY BEFORE deps.Git.UpdateRefCAS("HEAD",newSHA,parentSHA) (Gotcha #1; root passes expected=""). On CAS err → print §13.5 head-moved message via out.Progressf (HEAD moved; the generated message; the manual commit-tree command omitting -p when root) and return Result{},ErrHeadMoved (NEVER force, NEVER rescue).

In CommitStaged IMPLEMENT the FR42 SUCCESS print + return: short=newSHA (newSHA[:7] when len>7); out.Resultf("[%s] %s\n", short, firstLine(msg)); if dt,derr:=deps.Git.DiffTreeNameStatus(newSHA); derr==nil { out.Resultf("%s", dt) }; return Result{CommitSHA:newSHA, Subject:firstLine(msg), Message:msg}, nil. Add a code comment documenting that CommitStaged owns the success print (decisions.md §3 governs; Appendix D's 'main.go prints' is superseded).

CREATE internal/generate/generate_test.go (white-box `package generate`; stdlib + internal/* only; NO testify; t.Run table-driven; ui.NewOutput(&stdout,&stderr,false,true) so Red is a no-op). Add a stubGit implementing gitClient with canned returns + a call log, and a stubRunner implementing runner (Run returns canned stdout/error; Parse returns canned (msg,ok)).

In generate_test.go cover: happy-path (unique first try → Result, CommitTree+UpdateRefCAS once, no rescue); dup-retry-then-success (1st subject dup, 2nd unique → rejected grows, 2nd commits); parse-retry-then-success (1st Parse ok=false, corrective payload rebuilt, 2nd ok=true); parse-fail-after-inner (both Parse ok=false → Rescue(candidate="")+ErrRescue); dup-exhaustion (always dup, assert EXACTLY MaxDuplicateRetries+1 Run calls → Rescue(candidate=last msg)+ErrRescue); nothing-to-commit (diff=="" → ErrNothingToCommit, NO Rescue, NO WriteTree); WriteTree error (returned directly, no rescue); timeout (Run returns *provider.TimeoutError → Rescue+ErrRescue); signal-cancel (Run returns context.Canceled → ErrRescue, Rescue NOT called by CommitStaged); head-moved (UpdateRefCAS errors → §13.5 message+ErrHeadMoved, CommitTree WAS called, never force); root commit (RevParseHEAD hasParent=false → CommitTree/UpdateRefCAS called with "").

RUN the validation gates in order, fixing every failure before declaring done: go build ./internal/... ; go vet ./internal/... ; test -z "$(gofmt -l internal/)" ; go test ./internal/generate/ -run CommitStaged -v ; go test ./internal/git/ ./internal/provider/ -v ; go test ./...
Docs: this task has no Mode-A per-item DOCS line; it defers to the Mode-B changeset-level doc sync performed in M8 (README/docs). No doc file edit is required in this task.

successCriteria.description: internal/generate/generate.go exists, owns `// Package generate`, and exposes CommitStaged(ctx context.Context, deps Deps) (Result, error) plus the gitClient/runner interfaces, Deps, Result, and the three sentinel errors ErrNothingToCommit/ErrRescue/ErrHeadMoved.

CommitStaged takes a single Deps value (Git gitClient, Runner runner, Manifest, Config, Output *ui.Output) so every collaborator is injectable; *git.Git and *provider.Executor satisfy the interfaces structurally.

The two-nested-loop counting is EXACT: outer loop `dupAttempt := 0; dupAttempt <= cfg.MaxDuplicateRetries` (inclusive, 1 initial + N retries, default 4 total); inner loop a FRESH `parseAttempt := 1; parseAttempt <= 2` budget reset every outer iteration.

The corrective retry REBUILDS the payload via prompt.AssemblePayload(diff, instr, rejected) — the diff and growing rejected list STAY; only the instruction slot swaps to manifest.RetryInstruction (non-empty) else the original; instr is reset to the original at the top of each outer iteration.
CommitStaged RETURNS the sentinel errors and NEVER calls os.Exit; the CLI (P1.M7.T2) is expected to map them via errors.Is to exit codes.

Rescue candidate is msg ONLY on dup-exhaustion; "" on timeout/agent-error/parse-fail-after-inner and post-snapshot git errors; the signal-cancel path (errors.Is(runErr, context.Canceled)) returns ErrRescue WITHOUT rendering Rescue (the signal handler already did).
WriteTree error is returned directly (NOT ErrRescue — no tree was created); a diff=="" returns ErrNothingToCommit with NO Rescue and NO WriteTree call.
restoreSignalHandler() is called immediately BEFORE git.UpdateRefCAS and installSignalHandler immediately AFTER WriteTree, with the rescueFn closure gating `if treeSHA != ""` and candidate "".

On UpdateRefCAS failure, the §13.5 head-moved message + manual recovery command (omitting -p when root) is printed to stderr and ErrHeadMoved is returned; NEVER --force, never the 1-arg form except the root commit.
Root commit (RevParseHEAD hasParent=false ⇒ parentSHA="") is handled: CommitTree omits -p and UpdateRefCAS uses expected="".

The FR42 success block is printed to stdout via out.Resultf ([<short-sha>] <subject> then the git.DiffTreeNameStatus name-status lines) and Result{CommitSHA, Subject, Message} is returned; CommitStaged NEVER calls git add/AddAll/HasStagedChanges (v2 seam).
An exported (*provider.Executor).Parse(raw, m Manifest) (string, bool) is added additively to internal/provider/executor.go; parse.go and parse_test.go are untouched.

A read-only git.DiffTreeNameStatus(sha string) (string, error) is added to internal/git/plumbing.go running `git diff-tree --no-commit-id --name-status -r <sha>` via g.run, surfacing non-zero exits as *git.ExitError.

internal/generate/generate_test.go (white-box package generate, stubGit+stubRunner, no testify) covers happy-path, dup-retry-then-success, parse-retry-then-success, parse-fail-after-inner, dup-exhaustion (asserting exactly MaxDuplicateRetries+1 Run calls), nothing-to-commit, WriteTree error, timeout, signal-cancel, head-moved, and root-commit; dedupe.go/rescue.go/signal.go are untouched.
go build ./internal/..., go vet ./internal/..., gofmt (internal/ clean), and go test ./... all pass with no testify/external deps added.
Docs: task has no Mode-A DOCS line; defers to Mode-B changeset-level doc sync in M8 — no doc file edit required here.

references: plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/decisions.md §3 — ★ THE two-nested-loop CommitStaged contract ★ (ordered pseudocode; counting; COMMIT block; restore comment text says BEFORE update-ref)
plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/reference_impl.md §2 (two-nested-loop behavioral detail + off-by-one resolution), §3 (stdin payload ordering diff-first), §5 (rescue message baseline)
